articles.title: Silver rate today remains muted but in the red on firm dollar, dimming hopes of Fed rate cut amid ongoing US-Iran war
articles.description: On April 7, silver prices dipped 0.67% to ₹2,31,800 per kg amid geopolitical tensions in the Iran war and a firm dollar. Investors remain cautious ahead of U.S. inflation data and developments in the Strait of Hormuz, impacting both silver and gold markets.

articles.title: PPF interest rate 2026: Has Public Provident Fund interest rate increased for April
articles.description: The Public Provident Fund (PPF) interest rate remains unchanged at 7.1% per annum for the April-June 2026 quarter. The Finance Ministry confirmed that rates for small savings schemes will continue from the previous quarter. PPF offers tax benefits on contributions, interest, and withdrawals, making it a popular investment.

articles.title: Markets on Edge: U.S.-Iran Conflict Heightens Investor Uncertainty
articles.description: Global markets experienced volatility as tensions between the U.S. and Iran over the war escalate. The conflict has heightened oil prices and increased inflation concerns, driving investors to adopt a wait-and-see approach. President Donald Trump's deadline for a resolution looms, further stoking market anxiety.

articles.title: Gold steady as investors await Trump deadline on Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Gold prices held steady as global tensions simmered. Investors are keenly watching a U.S. deadline regarding the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial oil route. Meanwhile, differing views on inflation and interest rates from Federal Reserve officials are keeping markets on edge, with key U.S. inflation data on the horizon.

articles.title: Jim Cramer says potential stock market bottom is tied to interest rates, not war headlines
articles.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said investors shouldn't get comfortable calling a market bottom just yet, because the real driver of this market isn't geopolitics.

articles.title: Home insurance rates jump 46%, outpacing inflation nationwide
articles.description: If you think your budget has been tight lately, look at your insurance bill. A new report from LendingTree found that home insurance rates are rising.

articles.title: Inflation fears could push Fed to raise interest rates, key official says
articles.description: Amid fears that prices are starting to rise too quickly again, the Federal Reserve may opt to tap the brakes on inflation with a potential rate hike later this week, a top official warned.

articles.title: US sexual health report card: High pleasure, low testing, stark gender disparities
articles.description: A new sexual health survey reveals a mix of progress and persistent gaps. Overall, many Americans report positive experiences—interest in having sex, sexual pleasure, and good communication with partners—yet women and gender-diverse individuals still face higher rates of sexual violence, lower sexual pleasure, and discomfort discussing sexual health. The study was led by Jessie Ford, Ph.D., assistant professor of sociomedical sciences at Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health, and the findings are published in the Journal of Sex & Marital Therapy.

articles.title: Standard Capital Markets Completes ₹380 Crore NCD Redemptions Across Series
articles.description: Standard Capital Markets Limited executed total NCD redemptions of ₹380 crore through systematic debt management, completing Series III redemption of ₹130 crore on April 02, 2026, and finalizing Series I clearance worth ₹250 crore on April 06, 2026, with all redemptions including accrued interest and maintaining full regulatory compliance.

articles.title: Ceasefire Tensions Propel U.S. Stocks Amid Market Optimism
articles.description: U.S. stocks rose as investors monitored U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations and President Trump's aggressive rhetoric. Despite Iran's rejection of immediate ceasefire offers, markets showed optimism as talks continued. Investment sectors hope for a bullish earnings season, despite economic turmoil and inflation fears, as signs point to potential resolution.
